# TekMemo

> TekMemo is a file-first memory runtime for AI agents and coding assistants. It gives LLMs durable, project-scoped context so they stay accurate and consistent across sessions. Memory lives as plain text and JSON in a `.tekmemo/` folder (local) or in TekMemo Cloud (hosted).

TekMemo is published as focused packages:
- `@tekmemo/core` — the core memory runtime (the `Tekmemo` client, filesystem store, graph memory, recall, and cloud client contracts).
- `tekmemo` — the CLI for terminal and CI workflows.
- `@tekmemo/mcp-server` — a self-hostable MCP server (stdio) for agent integration.

Official adapters live alongside it:
- `@tekmemo/adapter-openai` — OpenAI embeddings.
- `@tekmemo/adapter-voyage` — Voyage AI embedder + reranker.
- `@tekmemo/adapter-transformers` — zero-config local ONNX embedder (Transformers.js) for hybrid recall with no API key, no cloud.

## Recall (local intelligence)

Local and hybrid modes retrieve memory through a configurable recall engine. In local mode, recall works with zero configuration: the default is lexical-only retrieval (BM25 + fuzzy matching), so memory is searchable with no embedder and no API keys. Strategies (`recall.engine`): `lexical`, `vector`, `hybrid`, or `auto` (default — `hybrid` when an embedder is available, else `lexical`). When `recall.localEmbeddings` is on, the runtime lazy-loads the local ONNX adapter so `auto` upgrades to hybrid recall with zero API keys. The vector path is an enhancement: if the embedder fails or the adapter is missing, writes and recall keep working on the lexical path.

Environment variables (same priority chain: constructor args > env > `.tekmemo/config.json` > defaults):
- `TEKMEMO_RECALL_ENGINE` — `lexical` | `vector` | `hybrid` | `auto` (default `auto`).
- `TEKMEMO_LOCAL_EMBEDDINGS` — `1`/`true` to lazy-load the local ONNX embedder.
- `TEKMEMO_EMBEDDING_MODEL` — Transformers.js-compatible model id (default `Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2`).

## Quick start

Install the core runtime and start recording memory in code:

```bash
npm install @tekmemo/core
```

```ts
import { Tekmemo } from "@tekmemo/core";
import { createNodeFsMemoryStore } from "@tekmemo/core/node-fs";

const memo = new Tekmemo({
  store: createNodeFsMemoryStore({ rootDir: "./.tekmemo" }),
  projectId: "my-app",
  mode: "local",
});

await memo.write({ content: "Use D1 for tenant metadata.", kind: "decision" });
const hits = await memo.recall({ query: "database decisions" });
```

## Docs

- Overview & quick start: https://docs.memo.tekbreed.com/packages/core/
- Installation: https://docs.memo.tekbreed.com/packages/cli/
- The `Tekmemo` client (modes, strategies, API surface): https://docs.memo.tekbreed.com/packages/core/client
- Configuration (resolution chain, env vars, config.json): https://docs.memo.tekbreed.com/packages/core/configuration
- Core concepts: https://docs.memo.tekbreed.com/packages/core/concepts
- Filesystem layout: https://docs.memo.tekbreed.com/packages/core/agentfs
- Cloud client / storage: https://docs.memo.tekbreed.com/configure/storage
- Architecture: https://docs.memo.tekbreed.com/packages/core/concepts

## Runtime modes

`Tekmemo` supports three runtime modes, selected in config: `local` (filesystem), `hybrid` (local + cloud with read/write policies), and `memory` (volatile, for tests).

- Local vs hybrid: https://docs.memo.tekbreed.com/packages/core/client
- Configuration resolution: https://docs.memo.tekbreed.com/packages/core/configuration

## MCP (Model Context Protocol)

Two ways to expose TekMemo to coding agents:

- **Hosted server** (cloud-only): `https://mcp.memo.tekbreed.com/` over Streamable HTTP, bearer-token auth, read-only by default. Docs: https://docs.memo.tekbreed.com/packages/mcp/
- **Self-hosted stdio server** (`@tekmemo/mcp-server`): supports `local`, `hybrid`, and `memory` modes for file-first memory. Docs: https://docs.memo.tekbreed.com/packages/mcp/

Client setup (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, etc.) for both paths: https://docs.memo.tekbreed.com/packages/mcp/

## CLI

- CLI guide: https://docs.memo.tekbreed.com/packages/cli/
- Commands: https://docs.memo.tekbreed.com/packages/cli/

## API reference

- Modules overview: https://docs.memo.tekbreed.com/api/
- Core client API: https://docs.memo.tekbreed.com/api/core
- AI SDK helpers: https://docs.memo.tekbreed.com/packages/adapters/ai-sdk

## Cloud

TekMemo Cloud base URL: `https://api.tekbreed.com/memo/v1`. Authenticate with a TekMemo API key (`tk_live_...`), kept server-side. Cloud is optional — local file-first memory works with zero cloud setup.
